title: Add wireframe terrain heightfield generator
overview: Create a pure data generator that produces an MxN planar grid mesh with Y-heights set by layered FBM noise (sin/cos based, consistent with codebase patterns). Outputs flat Float32Array positions and edge pairs for LineSegments rendering. Grid resolution adapts to quality tier. Deterministic per session seed.

steps.description: Add terrain quality fields to QualityProfile and wire into tier system
details: Add `terrainRows: number` and `terrainCols: number` to QualityProfile interface
Set tier values: low={terrainRows:20, terrainCols:30}, medium={terrainRows:40, terrainCols:60}, high={terrainRows:60, terrainCols:90}
Add 'terrainRows' and 'terrainCols' to COUNT_FIELDS array (10→12 entries)
Add COUNT_MINIMUMS entries: terrainRows:8, terrainCols:8
Add 'terrain' case to extractSystemConfig switch returning {rows: profile.terrainRows, cols: profile.terrainCols, noiseOctaves: profile.noiseOctaves}
Import createPRNG from '../prng' and hashRandomFromPosition from './subdivideEdges'
Export interface TerrainHeightfieldResult with: positions: Float32Array, randoms: Float32Array, edgeCount: number, rows: number, cols: number
Export function generateTerrainHeightfield(opts: { rows: number, cols: number, seed: string, width?: number, depth?: number, heightScale?: number, octaves?: number }): TerrainHeightfieldResult
Default extents: width=10, depth=10, heightScale=2.0, octaves=3
Create (rows+1)*(cols+1) vertex grid on XZ plane centered at origin

Implement seeded FBM height function using sin/cos layered noise (consistent with hashRandomFromPosition pattern): for each octave, accumulate amplitude * sin(freq*x*p1 + freq*z*p2 + seedOffset) * cos(freq*x*p3 + freq*z*p4 + seedOffset) with frequency doubling and amplitude halving per octave. Prime coefficients (73.1, 91.3, etc.) sourced per octave from seed via createPRNG
Vertex Y = fbm(x, z, seed, octaves) * heightScale
Compute edge count: rows*(cols+1) + cols*(rows+1) — horizontal edges + vertical edges
Build flat Float32Array for edge pairs: each edge = 6 floats (x0,y0,z0, x1,y1,z1). Horizontal edges connect (i,j)→(i,j+1), vertical edges connect (i,j)→(i+1,j)
Build randoms Float32Array using hashRandomFromPosition for each edge endpoint (edgeCount*2*3 floats)
All output deterministic for same seed+rows+cols

steps.description: Unit tests covering all acceptance criteria
details: Import generateTerrainHeightfield from source, describe block: 'US-073: Wireframe terrain heightfield generator'
T-073-01: Vertex count equals (rows+1)*(cols+1) — verify positions.length === vertexCount*6... actually positions is edge-pair based. Verify edge positions length equals edgeCount*2*3
T-073-02: Edge count equals rows*(cols+1) + cols*(rows+1) for multiple grid sizes
T-073-03: All position values are finite
T-073-04: All random values are finite and in [0, 1)
T-073-05: Randoms array length equals edgeCount * 2 * 3
T-073-06: Output is deterministic — same seed+params produces identical positions
T-073-07: Different seeds produce different positions
T-073-08: Heights (Y values) are not all zero — terrain has visible hills/valleys
T-073-09: Heights vary — min Y !== max Y, range is meaningful relative to heightScale
T-073-10: Grid extents — X positions span approximately [-width/2, width/2], Z spans approximately [-depth/2, depth/2]

implementation_notes.noise_approach: Sin/cos layered FBM using prime coefficients, consistent with hashRandomFromPosition pattern already in codebase. No external noise library needed. Seed-derived offsets via createPRNG ensure determinism and per-session variation.
implementation_notes.output_format: Flat Float32Array edge pairs matching polyhedraEdges/cubeLattice convention: positions has 6 floats per edge (2 endpoints × vec3), randoms has 6 floats per edge (hashRandomFromPosition per endpoint). This is directly consumable by Three.js LineSegments with BufferGeometry.
implementation_notes.quality_scaling: Grid resolution (rows×cols) scales via COUNT_FIELDS/scaleQualityProfile mechanism. Low tier ~20×30=600 cells, medium ~40×60=2400, high ~60×90=5400. COUNT_MINIMUMS floor at 8×8=64 cells.
implementation_notes.edge_formula: Total edges = rows*(cols+1) + cols*(rows+1). Horizontal: (rows+1) rows of cols edges each = cols*(rows+1). Vertical: (cols+1) columns of rows edges each = rows*(cols+1).

verification.build: npm run build must pass with no type errors
verification.tests: npx vitest run tests/visual/generators/terrainHeightfield.test.ts — all 10 tests pass
